result:
Q1 integrated (Clarifications + FR-011a + SC-004 updated).

---

**Q2: Pinned canonical sources for the contested programs**

The brief flags nSuns and GZCLP as having multiple variants and says to "pin a canonical source per program." This is the single largest acceptance surface — every per-program progression test asserts against whatever variant we pin, so the choice directly shapes the program engine and its tests.

**Recommended:** Option A — pin the most widely-circulated community-canonical variants. These are what the target user has actually seen/run, are well-documented, and give unambiguous test oracles.

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| A | Pin community-standard variants: **nSuns** = the 9-set 5/3/1 LP (T1 + T2, AMRAP-driven TM bump); **GZCLP** = Cody Lefever's base LP (T1 5×3→6×2→10×1, T2 3×10→3×8→3×6, T3 3×15+ AMRAP); **5/3/1** = Wendler 2nd-ed with optional AMRAP; **Madcow** = standard 5×5 weekly ramp; **PPL** = Metallicadpa Reddit PPL |
| B | Pin the same set, but **5/3/1 with FSL** (First Set Last) assistance instead of BBB |
| C | You specify exact sources/variants per program (≤5 words each) |
| Short | Different single guiding source (≤5 words) |

You can reply with the option letter, accept the recommendation ("yes"/"recommended"), or provide your own short answer.
